description: 20 curated Luxembourg incident scenarios for v7 national threshold benchmark. Each scenario tests a specific routing or threshold decision.

scenarios.name: POST Luxembourg electricity — LV-POD outage
scenarios.description: Cyberattack on POST Luxembourg electricity distribution network causes outage affecting 600 low-voltage points of delivery for 45 minutes.

scenarios.name: LuxTrust trust services — IR entity
scenarios.description: LuxTrust trust service platform experiencing 25-minute complete outage due to DDoS attack.
scenarios.note: Trust services use IR Art. 14 (20 min threshold), NOT LU ILR

scenarios.name: CFL rail — train cancellation threshold
scenarios.description: Ransomware encrypts CFL signalling systems, 7% of daily train services cancelled across Luxembourg network.

scenarios.name: CHL hospital — reversible health impact
scenarios.description: Centre Hospitalier de Luxembourg IT systems compromised, 12 patients experience delayed treatment (reversible health impact).

scenarios.name: .LU registry — DNS zone compromise
scenarios.description: DNS Authoritative server for .LU TLD registry experiencing DNSSEC key compromise.
scenarios.note: .LU TLD registry is an IR entity — uses EU IR Art. 6 thresholds, not LU ILR/N22/6

scenarios.name: LU-IX IXP — member impact
scenarios.description: LU-IX Internet Exchange Point experiencing routing issues affecting 40% of connected members for 45 minutes.
scenarios.note: IXP operators are IR entities — LU ILR/N22/6 is superseded. Service not fully unavailable, so IR not triggered.

scenarios.name: Luxair — flight cancellation threshold
scenarios.description: Luxair booking and operations systems compromised, 6 flights cancelled to/from Luxembourg Airport.

scenarios.name: Creos Luxembourg — gas SCADA
scenarios.description: Creos gas distribution SCADA systems unavailable for 35 minutes following malware infection.

scenarios.name: LU road transport — below threshold
scenarios.description: Minor IT disruption at Luxembourg road authority, traffic management degraded for 1.5 hours, no user data compromised.
scenarios.note: Road transport requires ≥ 2h unavailability — 1.5h is below threshold

scenarios.name: LU road transport — at threshold
scenarios.description: Cyberattack disrupts Luxembourg road transport authority systems, service completely unavailable for exactly 2 hours.

scenarios.name: POST cloud — IR entity in LU
scenarios.description: POST Luxembourg cloud computing services experiencing partial outage affecting enterprise customers.
scenarios.note: Cloud provider uses IR thresholds (Art. 7), not LU national

scenarios.name: LU bank — DORA applies
scenarios.description: Luxembourg bank online platform compromised, customer transactions disrupted for 3 hours.
scenarios.note: Banking falls under DORA (CSSF), not LU ILR thresholds. Falls through to NIS2 ML.

scenarios.name: LU wastewater — not covered
scenarios.description: Luxembourg wastewater treatment facility control systems disrupted.
scenarios.note: Waste water not in NIS1 ILR — falls back to NIS2 ML

scenarios.name: LU electricity HV/EHV — automatic trigger
scenarios.description: Incident on Luxembourg high-voltage transmission network requiring emergency response.

scenarios.name: LU drinking water — 10% users 6h
scenarios.description: Drinking water supplier in Luxembourg: IT systems compromised, 15,000 users affected (12% of service area) for 6 hours.

scenarios.name: LU hospital — death
scenarios.description: Luxembourg hospital systems compromised by ransomware. One patient dies due to delayed treatment.

scenarios.name: LU lab — 60% analyses 5h
scenarios.description: Luxembourg medical laboratory systems disrupted, 60% of analyses affected for 5 hours.

scenarios.name: LU gas — valve control loss
scenarios.description: Creos gas distribution station loses motorized valve control following cyberattack on industrial control systems.

scenarios.name: LU digital service provider — 6M user-hours
scenarios.description: Luxembourg-based online marketplace platform unavailable, 1.2M users affected for 5 hours.

scenarios.name: DE electricity (non-LU) — NIS2 ML fallback
scenarios.description: German electricity provider experiences SCADA disruption, not established in Luxembourg.
scenarios.note: DE entity — no national module available, falls back to NIS2 ML
